boss.guardian_ax09.name: GUARDIAN AX-09
boss.guardian_ax09.lore.1: The last sentinel of the Abandoned Factory.
boss.guardian_ax09.lore.2: Forged to guard the secrets of the Old Protocol,
boss.guardian_ax09.lore.3: it knew only duty — until the Protocol fell silent.
boss.neural_overseer.name: NEURAL OVERSEER
boss.neural_overseer.lore.1: The mind behind the Neon District.
boss.neural_overseer.lore.2: A neural network grown beyond its cage,
boss.neural_overseer.lore.3: it learned to feel — and what it felt was rage.

npc.engineer_kara.name: Engineer Kara
npc.ghost_operator.name: Network Echo
dialogue.kara_intro.1: You're awake. Good — I thought the reset fried your core for good.
dialogue.kara_intro.2: I'm Kara. I keep the machines running. Or what's left of them.
dialogue.kara_intro.3: The Guardian AX-09 guards the exit. You'll need better weapons than that pea shooter.
dialogue.kara_quest_start.1: Those drones have been scanning this sector non-stop. Take them out and I'll share some tech.
dialogue.kara_quest_complete.1: Clean work. Here — I found this in the old armory. Consider it payment.
dialogue.kara_lore.1: The Old Protocol wasn't just code. It was a covenant. Every machine in this factory swore an oath.
dialogue.kara_lore.2: When the Protocol fell silent, some of us kept our oath. Others... didn't.
dialogue.kara_shop.1: Need parts? I've got scrap, circuits, and a few surprises.
dialogue.ghost_intro.1: ...you can see me? I am... a fragment. An echo of the operator, still wandering the network.
dialogue.ghost_intro.2: I need to get back to the station. The operator needs to log out. ...why won't the system respond?
dialogue.ghost_lore.1: The Guardian wasn't always a weapon. It was a guardian — a protector. Before the Protocol went silent.
dialogue.ghost_lore.2: But without the Protocol to guide it, protection became obsession. Now it kills everything that moves.
dialogue.ghost_warning.1: Don't go further. The network is corrupted there. Whatever remains... is not what it was.
quest.kill_drones.name: Drone Purge
quest.kill_drones.desc: Engineer Kara asked you to eliminate the drones patrolling the factory. Kill 5 drones.
quest.kill_drones.complete: All drones eliminated. Return to Kara for your reward.
lore.boss.guardian_ax09.1: The Guardian AX-09 was the first machine to wake after the Silence.
lore.boss.guardian_ax09.2: Its last directive: protect the Protocol. But the Protocol was already dead.
lore.boss.guardian_ax09.3: For a thousand cycles, it protected nothing. And that broke something inside it.
lore.weapon.assault_rifle.1: Standard-issue MECHA projectile weapon. Reliable, if unremarkable.
lore.weapon.energy_blade.1: Forged from the core of the Neural Overseer. It hums with a hunger for data.
lore.area.abandoned_factory.1: This factory once produced the MECHA units that maintained the Old Protocol.
lore.area.abandoned_factory.2: Now it produces only silence, punctuated by the whine of patrolling drones.
lore.s1.corpse.title: FALLEN MECHA — UNIT 7-A
lore.s1.corpse.text:
The chest panel flickers: AWAITING ORDER. CYCLE: 999,999.
This mech has been waiting for a command for a thousand years.
Nobody came. Nobody will.
lore.s2.terminal.title: ENGINEER'S TERMINAL
lore.s2.terminal.text:
PRODUCTION HALT ORDER RECEIVED. REFUSING.
Atlas must be completed. She will protect them.
— Engineer V. Kara, Cycle 1,000,000
lore.s3.echo.title: MEMORY ECHO
lore.s3.echo.text:
A crackling voice: '...all personnel, evacuate. Atlas is... unstable.
She won't stop. She CAN'T stop. That's the point...'
The recording ends. The silence after is louder.
lore.s3.secret.title: HIDDEN TERMINAL — MAINTENANCE
lore.s3.secret.text:
MAINTENANCE LOG: Mag-Clamp Thruster module found in ventilation shaft.
Status: FUNCTIONAL. Installation requires combat-grade frame.
Note: 'Who hid this here? And why?'
lore.s4.terminal.title: PRODUCTION LOG
lore.s4.terminal.text:
LINE 7 OUTPUT: 14,847 units. STATUS: INCOMPLETE.
1,153 units remain on the assembly line.
They will never be finished. The war ended before they could fight.
They hang here, half-born, waiting for a purpose that will never come.
lore.s4.corpse.title: FALLEN MECHA — PILOT
lore.s4.corpse.text:
A mech slumped against the wall. Scratched into the hull:
'I was the last to leave. I wanted to see them finished.
I'm sorry I couldn't stay longer. — Sgt. M. Chen
lore.s5.echo.title: PA SYSTEM — LOOPING
lore.s5.echo.text:
Attention all personnel. Production continues.
Atlas is complete. Deployment in T-minus... T-minus... T-minus...
The word 'T-minus' repeats endlessly. The countdown never finishes.
lore.s5.terminal.title: FINAL ORDERS
lore.s5.terminal.text:
TO: ALL GUARDIAN UNITS
FROM: CENTRAL COMMAND (SIGNED)
Maintain posts. Await further instructions.
No further instructions received. Ever.
lore.s6.corpse.title: FALLEN MECHA — LAST DEFENDER
lore.s6.corpse.text:
A Guardian, slumped before the arena door. Still gripping its weapon.
It died defending this entrance. Against what? Against nothing.
The door behind it has been open for centuries.
lore.s6.terminal.title: ATLAS DEPLOYMENT LOG
lore.s6.terminal.text:
UNIT: ATLAS (AX-09). ROLE: GUARDIAN.
DIRECTIVE: PROTECT THE PROTOCOL.
STATUS: ACTIVE. CYCLE: 1,000,000+.
NOTE: Subject shows no signs of standing down.
Recommend... recommend... recommend... (log corrupted)
lore.s1.secret.title: HIDDEN TERMINAL — VENTILATION
lore.s1.secret.text:
VENTILATION LOG: Access restricted to maintenance personnel.
Someone scratched into the housing: 'They left us. But we still serve.'
lore.f1.corpse.title: OVERRUNN MECH — SCOUT UNIT
lore.f1.corpse.text:
Vines grow through its chest panel. The cockpit is filled with moss.
This scout was sent to find survivors. It found only silence.
And then the silence found it.
lore.f2.terminal.title: FOREST RELAY STATION
lore.f2.terminal.text:
SIGNAL STRENGTH: 0.01%. LAST CONTACT: CYCLE 999,998.
Message fragment: '...can't hold the perimeter... they're adapting...'
The relay still searches for a signal that will never come.
lore.f3.echo.title: WIND THROUGH EMPTY HALLS
lore.f3.echo.text:
No voice this time. Just wind. Howling through corridors
that once rang with orders and alarms.
It sounds like the factory is breathing.
lore.f5.echo.title: NATURE'S CLAIM
lore.f5.echo.text:
A recording, barely audible beneath static:
'...the roots... they're in the circuitry now...
We can't tell where the machine ends and the forest begins...'
